zh.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson於大約1265年出生在蘇格蘭。他於大約1285年與Mother of Isobel Davidson在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世在他的家鄉，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson于大约1265年出生在苏格兰。他于大约1285年与Mother of Isobel Davidson在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世在他的家乡，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Davidson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, northern English, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name David + -son. As a Jewish name, the last element comes from German Sohn ‘son’.
Irish (Down) and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt .
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Davidsson: patronymic from the personal name David .

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

nameMeanings.name: Father
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English fader, father, feder, fether (Old English fæder) ‘father’, often used to denote someone who exercised protecting care like that of a father. Some examples may belong at (2).
2 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Fader (Old Danish Fathir, Old Swedish Fadhir). Some early bearers may belong with (1).

variants.name: Feather
meanings: From the vocabulary word denoting the hardy, brightly coloured plant (Middle English hather; the spelling was altered in the 18th century as a result of folk etymological association with heath). The name was first used in the late 19th century and became particularly popular from the mid-1940s.

variants.name: Fathers
meanings: English: variant of Father with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Isobel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson was born about 1265, in Scotland. He married Mother of Isobel Davidson about 1285, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in his hometown, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson est né environ 1265, à Écosse. Il a épousé Mother of Isobel Davidson environ 1285, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson wurde um 1265 in Scotland geboren. Er hat um 1285 in Scotland Mother of Isobel Davidson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson è nato il circa 1265 nel Scotland. Lui sposato Mother of Isobel Davidson circa 1285, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidsonは約1265年にスコットランドで生まれた。約1285年にスコットランドでMother of Isobel Davidsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は故郷で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson(은)는 대략 1265년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1285년 스컷랜드에서 Mother of Isobel Davidson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson nasceu aproximadamente 1265, em Scotland. Ele casou-se com Mother of Isobel Davidson aproximadamente 1285, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson родился около 1265 г. в местности Шотландия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mother of Isobel Davidson около 1285 г. в местности Шотландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Father of Isobel Davidson nació aproximadamente 1265, en Scotland. Se casó con Mother of Isobel Davidson aproximadamente 1285, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
